_comment: Generated by kitsoki persona-qa deck from tools/persona_qa/examples/runs/kitsoki-product-review (persona-qa-kitsoki-product-review). Re-run the command instead of hand-editing derived scenes.

scenes.narration: This deck was generated deterministically from an existing Persona QA run bundle and its recorded media manifest.
scenes.subtitle: Kitsoki - Software architect - persona-qa-kitsoki-product-review
scenes.title: Kitsoki Persona QA Example

scenes.body:
Description: Designs delivery systems and frequently presents technical decisions to engineers and leadership.
Surface preference: web-first review with terminal verification
Risk focus: evidence traceability, workflow legibility, operational handoff
Starting surface: Open the product surface first, then inspect the generated artifacts.
First question: Can I explain the delivery loop to another architect without rerunning the system?
Evidence emphasis: Recorded UI playback, scenario outcomes, review gates, and reproducible commands.
Escalation trigger: A claim without playback or a workflow step that cannot be traced to source artifacts.
Finding bias: Prefer findings that improve reviewability and presentation readiness.
scenes.narration: The deck keeps the persona lens visible so reviewers judge the scenario evidence from the intended point of view.

scenes.caption: Rows are sorted by scenario id for deterministic output.
scenes.narration: The scenario table ties each natural-use task to its outcome, evidence coverage, and playback count.
scenes.title: Recorded scenarios

scenes.body:
Status: ready
Summary: Kitsoki can produce a presentation-ready journey deck when scenarios, review gates, and recorded playback are kept together.
Scenarios: 2
Evidence present: 5/5
Proof evidence: 2
pass: architect-readiness - The generated deck explains the workflow in reviewable chunks.
pass: playback-evidence - Each scenario has a playback-capable media manifest item.
pass: regeneration - Repeated persona-qa deck generation is byte-stable.
pass: scenario-source - The deck source is a committed run bundle fixture.
scenes.narration: The review scene summarizes bundle readiness without rerunning capture or model work.

scenes.caption:
explain-delivery-loop: validated
verify-ui-evidence: validated
scenes.narration: Findings are grouped by type so a technical reviewer can separate strengths from product risks.
scenes.title: Persona judgement

scenes.caption:
Playback items: 2
Videos: 2
Images: 0
Scenarios: explain-delivery-loop, verify-ui-evidence

items.detail: Recorded rrweb clip used as reviewable Kitsoki UI playback evidence.
items.label: explain-delivery-loop

items.detail: Recorded rrweb clip showing the verified UI state after the issue is addressed.
items.label: verify-ui-evidence

scenes.narration: The media scene carries structured playback references so Slidey can render the recorded journey clips.
scenes.title: Playback manifest

scenes.body:
explain-delivery-loop: rrweb-replay / kitsoki web
verify-ui-evidence: rrweb-replay / kitsoki web
event kitsoki-001: Opened Kitsoki UI playback and attached the delivery-loop clip.
event kitsoki-002: Compared the verification clip against the review gate.
scenes.narration: The driver scene records the capture harness and journal trail behind the visible playback evidence.

scenes.caption: Recorded rrweb clip used as reviewable Kitsoki UI playback evidence.
scenes.narration: Recorded playback evidence for explain-delivery-loop / key_interaction_video.
scenes.title: explain-delivery-loop / key_interaction_video

scenes.caption: Recorded rrweb clip showing the verified UI state after the issue is addressed.
scenes.narration: Recorded playback evidence for verify-ui-evidence / key_interaction_video.
scenes.title: verify-ui-evidence / key_interaction_video

scenes.body:
Run bundle: tools/persona_qa/examples/runs/kitsoki-product-review
Playback scenes: 2
Regenerate: kitsoki persona-qa deck --run-dir <run-dir> --out <deck.slidey.json>
Publish: commit the deck JSON; bundle it for the product-site gallery when it lives under docs/decks/.
scenes.narration: The deck is a derived artifact. The durable source is the run bundle plus the recorded media manifest.
